steps.title: Group the variable terms
steps.content:
Move the constant to the other side and group the $x$-terms and $y$-terms:
$$x^2+9x+y^2-7y=-11$$

steps.title: Complete both squares
steps.content:
Add the squares of half the linear coefficients to both sides:
$$x^2+9x+(\frac{9}{2})^2+y^2-7y+(\frac{7}{2})^2=-11+\frac{81}{4}+\frac{49}{4}$$

steps.title: Find the radius
steps.content:
Rewrite the left side as squared binomials and simplify the right side:
$$(x+\frac{9}{2})^2+(y-\frac{7}{2})^2=\frac{86}{4}$$
The radius is the square root of the right side:
$$r=\sqrt{\frac{86}{4}}=\frac{\sqrt{86}}{2}$$
The answer is A.
